talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Understanding Disseminated Tumor Cells: An Emerging Opportunity to Prevent Lethal Breast Cancer?
talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting Dormant Residual Tumor Cells in the Surveillance Window (CLEVER Trial)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we were told this is an impossible trial to do. No one will do it, no one will fund it."
Speaker said: "there were no unexpected adverse events, and there was no grade four or five toxicity."
Speaker said: "the five‑year recurrence‑free survival across all groups was about 96%, which is substantially better than would be expected based on the high‑risk nature of this population."

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: autophagy and mTOR signaling in dormant residual tumor cells
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Eligibility required bone‑marrow aspirate positivity by low‑sensitivity IHC, potentially excluding patients with low‑level DTCs and leading to selection bias
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: A high‑sensitivity, quantitative DTC biomarker (e.g., ctDNA or flow‑cytometry‑based assay) to stratify patients
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: The trial was not powered for recurrence‑free survival, making the exploratory endpoint underpowered

key_data_gaps: No biomarker‑stratified analysis beyond IHC DTC counts
No comparison to standard of care arm

crispro_opportunity.description: The current IHC‑based DTC assay is low‑throughput and insensitive; CrisPRO could develop a computational pipeline to integrate flow‑cytometry and ctDNA data for more precise DTC quantification.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "it's low throughput, it's low sensitivity... there is a high false negative rate from studying just one bone marrow aspirate"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply machine‑learning models to multimodal single‑cell and ctDNA datasets to generate a high‑sensitivity DTC risk score.

crispro_opportunity.description: Autophagy and mTOR inhibition reduce DTC burden; CrisPRO could identify patients whose DTCs show transcriptional signatures of autophagy dependence for targeted therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "pharmacologically block autophagy by administering chloroquine... results in an improvement in recurrence‑free survival"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to predict autophagy‑dependency scores from bulk and single‑cell RNA‑seq of DTCs, guiding chloroquine‑based regimens.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a biomarker‑driven adaptive trial that enrolls only patients with confirmed DTC clearance after a run‑in period, reducing dilution of signal.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the lessons of the CLEVER trials was first, we see concordant effects in patients than in mice... transient inhibition of autophagy or mTOR during this surveillance period can both reduce RTC burden"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO predictive models to define optimal treatment windows and adaptive randomization rules based on real‑time DTC dynamics.

unresolved_questions: Will long‑term follow‑up confirm a survival benefit of DTC‑targeted therapy?
What is the optimal duration of autophagy/mTOR inhibition to maximize benefit while minimizing toxicity?

talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Understanding Disseminated Tumor Cells: An Emerging Opportunity to Prevent Lethal Breast Cancer?
talk_metadata.talk_title: Residual Breast Cancer Cells Co‑opt SOX5‑driven Endochondral Ossification to Maintain Dormancy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "loss of SOX5 accelerates recurrence by increasing proliferation in the latent window"
Speaker said: "SOX5 loss massively decreased the calcification and PET tracer uptake that we normally see in the residual lesions"
Speaker said: "enrichment of these signatures was associated with a delay in time to recurrence"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Downstream effectors of SOX5 that enforce dormancy (e.g., specific osteogenic transcriptional programs) are not targeted by current therapies

key_data_gaps: No in‑human therapeutic intervention targeting SOX5 reported
Limited patient cohort linking ossification signatures to outcomes

crispro_opportunity.description: Endochondral ossification gene signatures could serve as a dormancy biomarker; CrisPRO can develop a predictive model to identify patients with high ossification scores for tailored therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "enrichment of these signatures was associated with a delay in time to recurrence"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate bulk RNA‑seq and single‑cell data to compute an ossification‑dormancy score for risk stratification.

crispro_opportunity.description: Loss of SOX5 removes a brake on proliferation; CrisPRO could screen for small molecules that mimic SOX5 activity or activate downstream osteogenic pathways.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "loss of SOX5 promotes RTC proliferation and accelerates recurrence"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational docking and network analysis to prioritize compounds that activate SOX5‑regulated osteogenic targets.

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO’s single‑cell multi‑omics pipeline can simultaneously capture ossification signatures and proliferation markers, outperforming standard IHC.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we used PET/CT with 18F‑sodium fluoride to detect calcification in residual lesions"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO to integrate imaging‑derived quantitative PET data with transcriptomics for a composite dormancy index.

unresolved_questions: What are the direct transcriptional targets of SOX5 that enforce the ossification program?
Can pharmacologic activation of the ossification pathway be safely achieved in patients?

talk_metadata.session_title: Advances in Understanding Disseminated Tumor Cells: An Emerging Opportunity to Prevent Lethal Breast Cancer?
talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular Characteristics of Patient‑Derived Disseminated Tumor Cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "dissemination actually starts very early, long before we have a tumor"
Speaker said: "M0 DCCs cluster very closely to human embryonic stem cells"
Speaker said: "embryo‑like DCCs are associated with risk of metastasis"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of sensitive detection methods for embryonic‑like DCCs during surveillance

key_data_gaps: No prospective clinical trial linking embryonic‑like DCC detection to therapeutic intervention
Limited longitudinal sampling to track DCC evolution

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a computational classifier to identify embryonic‑like DCC transcriptional signatures in bone‑marrow or blood samples.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "M0 DCCs actually cluster very closely to human embryonic stem cells"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Train a machine‑learning model on single‑cell RNA‑seq data to generate an embryonic‑DCC score for early risk assessment.

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO’s single‑cell phospho‑proteomics can uncover active signaling (e.g., IL‑6, p‑STAT3) in rare DCCs, surpassing bulk assays.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we can detect activation of HER2, phospho‑AKT, phospho‑STAT3 in individual DCCs"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate phospho‑protein barcoding with transcriptomics to map functional states of DCCs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify kinase inhibitors (e.g., JAK/STAT blockers) that target activated pathways in embryonic‑like DCCs for combination trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we see activation of IL6 signaling and phospho‑ERK, phospho‑AKT, phospho‑STAT3"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO network analysis to prioritize existing drugs that disrupt these signaling hubs in DCCs.

unresolved_questions: What drives the early embryonic‑like transcriptional program in M0 DCCs?
Why do embryonic‑like DCCs correlate with early mortality in lung cancer but later mortality in breast cancer?
